songs.title: H-H-Hat
songs.lyrics:
[Intro — whispered]
H! H! H-h-h!
(hh… hh… hh…)

[Hook]
H! H! H-h-h!
What begins with H?
H-h-hat! H-h-hat!
It's a hat! (a hat!)

[Verse 2]
H! H! H-h-h!
What begins with H?
H-h-hand! H-h-hand!
It's a hand! (a hand!)

[Verse 3]
H! H! H-h-h!
What begins with H?
H-h-horse! H-h-horse!
It's a horse! (a horse!)

[Verse 4]
H! H! H-h-h!
What begins with H?
H-h-house! H-h-house!
It's a house! (a house!)

[Bridge]
A potato?! (NO!)
P-p-potato! Not H!
(hh… hh… hh…)

[Outro]
H-h-hat! H-h-hat!
It's a hat! (a hat!)
H! H! H-h-h!
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 65-72 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: The H sound is a soft breath /h/ — a gentle warm exhale, like fogging a window, NEVER 'aitch' (letter name) and NEVER 'huh' (schwa). The '(hh…)' lines are quiet breathy pulses over the 808. Answer line 'It's a ___!' reuses the Week 1 frame.

songs.title: A Hat for the Ant
songs.lyrics:
[Intro — whispered]
(a hat… a hat… whose hat…)

[Hook]
A hat! A hat!
A hat for the ant!
The hat is on the ant! (the ant?!)
The ant is in the hat!

[Verse 2]
A hat! A hat!
A hat for the cat!
The hat is on the cat! (the cat?!)
The cat is in the hat!

[Verse 3]
A hat! A hat!
A hat for Sam!
The hat is on Sam! (it's Sam!)
Sam is in the hat!

[Bridge]
Who is in the hat?
(pat, pat, pat!)
The ant is in the hat!

[Outro]
A hat for the ant.
(shhh… the ant can nap in the hat…)
A hat for a POTATO?!
(PO-TA-TO!)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 65-72 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: One frame: 'A hat for the ___.' — the word 'for' is heard-only here (never printed), a natural connector. One hat gets tried on the whole cast; only the tiny ant can climb inside it. Kids choir answers the (…?!) echoes; whisper the outro before the potato hits.

spreads.artNote: One handsome little hat alone in the spotlight — the star object of the book. Whose hat is it?

spreads.text: The hat is on the cat.
spreads.artNote: The hat perched on the tabby cat's head, far too small — comically balanced between the ears.
The hat now on Sam the peg-doll boy, sliding down over his painted eyes — too big for a peg doll.

spreads.text: The hat is on the ant!
spreads.artNote: The hat drops over the tiny ant and the ant VANISHES completely underneath it — just a hat sitting on the floor. Funny mystery.

spreads.text: The ant is in the hat!
spreads.artNote: Cutaway reveal — the tiny ant standing happily INSIDE the huge hat like a domed room. The hat is the ant's whole house.

spreads.text: Is the ant in the hat?
spreads.artNote: The hat sits still on the floor; a tiny muffled speech-bubble squiggle from inside. Sam, Segina and the cat lean in, curious.

spreads.text: The ant can nap in the hat.
spreads.artNote: Cosy cutaway — the ant curled asleep inside the hat, which has become a snug little home. Warm, tender.

spreads.text: Pat the hat. The ant is in it!
spreads.artNote: A gentle hand patting the top of the hat; the ant peeks out from the brim, waving. Everyone delighted.
The potato now wears the hat, deadpan and dapper. The gag noun is left blank — the class SHOUTS 'POTATO' from the picture. The ant, evicted, stands beside it, arms crossed.

sentences: The hat is on the ant.
The ant is in the hat!
The cat sat on the ham!
extras: Moveable alphabet: build h-a-t, h-i-t, h-a-m, h-i-m, h-i-p
Sandpaper letter 'h' + sand tray — the soft breath /h/ (fog a little mirror)
'A hat for the ___' dress-up game: try one hat on cat / Sam / ant figures, read who it fits
Ant's-hat-house craft: a paper hat that opens to reveal the tiny ant inside

teacherFocus: Teach the soft breath /h/ — no voice at all, just warm air (fog a little mirror to feel it), never 'huh'. Blend h-a-t, h-i-t, h-a-m, h-i-m. The book is a dress-up joke — one hat tried on the whole cast, too small for the cat, too big for Sam, until the tiny ant discovers it makes the perfect little house. The word 'for' appears only in the sung title and the dress-up chant (heard, never printed). End tender: the ant napping inside its hat-home, then the potato steals the hat.
